Thought 4: The core contradiction is that Lloyd claims he and Russell were alone at the cabin, while Naomi claims she was alone at the cabin and Russell came to visit her. These two statements are mutually exclusive. The narrative provides a crucial detail that resolves this: Winston sees security footage of Naomi digging in her garden at her home the day before the murder. This means Naomi was not at the cabin "all weekend" as she claimed; she was at her home gardening. Her alibi is false. Furthermore, she states Russell came to the cabin to visit her, but if she wasn't there, this is also a lie. This makes her the most likely murderer, as she has both lied about her whereabouts and had access to the shovel (the murder weapon) through her gardening.
